That keeps duration-sensitive semis under macro pressure while leaving the higher-yield backdrop supportive for financials. Watch-only.

This eases the immediate inflation and rates pressure versus Monday's spike, but it leaves the nuclear, defense, and event-risk watch lanes live. Watch-only.

Watch-only held-name monitoring: single-name recovery improved, yet sector hedge pressure was still live into Friday.

signal_log.signal: BMNR preferred financing adds weekly cash-burden and forced-funding risk
signal_log.note: SEC 8-K filed June 10, 2026 says Bitmine issued 3.5M shares of 9.50% Series A perpetual preferred for about $273.8M net proceeds to fund ETH/digital-asset strategy, staking infrastructure, strategic investments, working capital, and common repurchases. The terms add cumulative weekly cash dividends; after a deferral the company must use commercially reasonable efforts over the following 30 days to sell common stock, other securities, and/or digital assets to cover arrears. A June 12 follow-up declared the first cash dividends and said BMNP trading is expected to begin June 16. This matters to the BMNR special re-check and lowers conviction for any immediate add until fresh ETH deployment, common/preferred price reaction, and cleaner flow confirm the financing is accretive rather than overhang. Window: June 16 listing and June 22/26 dividend dates. Disconfirm if post-raise holdings deployment and price/flow turn clearly constructive. Missing: updated ETH holdings, cash-burden absorption, and live common/preferred reaction.

signal_log.signal: XOP UW flow leaned bearish despite the Hormuz oil-risk backdrop
signal_log.note: Unusual Whales June 12 flow-recent for XOP showed 50 recent prints with 28 bearish tags versus 20 bullish and 35 bid-side versus 13 ask-side. XLE ETF tide into 15:59 ET also showed negative net call premium (-$5.54M) and negative net put premium (-$2.66M), which is not a clean bullish confirmation of chasing the energy sleeve on geopolitics alone. This matters to the active Middle East oil/rates event-risk lane and the current XOP sell-fast posture: it supports keeping risk tight and treating weekend oil headlines as re-check inputs rather than automatic add signals. Window: next session. Disconfirm if Monday crude, XOP/XLE flow, and traffic headlines flip decisively bullish. Missing: Monday open tape, direct PortWatch time-series read, and same-session oil/rates reaction.
